lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you look after my crops for me?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: I might - which patch were you thinking of?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: You don't have any seeds planted in that patch. Plant some and I might agree to look after it for you.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: That patch is diseased - you should cure it before asking me to be responsible for it!

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: That patch is dead - it's too late for me to do anything about it now.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: That patch is already fully grown! I don't know what you want me to do with it!

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: I'm already looking after that patch for you.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: If you like, but I want [payment] for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't have any of those at the moment.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, it's a deal.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: [That'll do nicely, sir/That'll do nicely, madam/That'll do nicely]. Leave it with me - I'll make sure that patch grows for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's too much.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Well, I'm not wasting my time for free.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Poison ivy bushes don't get diseased, so you needn't worry about having it protected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me any farming advice?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: You can put up to ten potatoes, cabbages or onions in vegetable sacks, although you can't have a mix in the same sack.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: You can put up to five tomatoes, strawberries, apples, bananas or oranges into a fruit basket, although you can't have a mix in the same basket.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: You can buy all the farming tools from farming shops, which can be found close to the allotments.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: You can fill plantpots with soil from any empty patch, if you have a gardening trowel.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Tree seeds must be grown in a plantpot of soil into a sapling, and then transferred to a tree patch to continue growing to adulthood.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: If you want to make your own sacks and baskets you'll need to use the loom that's near the Farming shop in Falador. If you're a good enough [craftswoman/craftsman], that is.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: You don't have to buy all your plantpots you know, you can make them yourself on a pottery wheel. If you're a good enough [craftswoman/craftsman], that is.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Don't just throw away your weeds after you've raked a patch - put them in a compost bin and make some compost.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Applying compost to a patch will not only reduce the chance that your crops will get diseased, but you will also grow more crops to harvest.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Supercompost is far better than normal compost, but more expensive to make. You need to rot the right type of item; show me an item, and I'll tell you if it's super- compostable or not.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Vegetables, hops and flowers need constant watering - if you ignore my advice, you will sooner or later find yourself in possession of a dead farming patch.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: The only way to cure a bush or tree of disease is to prune away the diseased leaves with a pair of secateurs. For all other crops I would just apply some plant-cure.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: There are five other Farming areas like this one - Elstan's area near Falador, Dantaera's area near Catherby, Kragen's area near Ardougne, Lyra's area in Morytania, Marisi's area in Hosidius and Oswallt's area

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: in Prifddinas.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: If you want to grow fruit trees you could try a few places: Catherby, Brimhaven, Gnome Stronghold and the Farming Guild all have fruit tree patches.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Hops are good for brewing ales. I believe there's a brewery up in Keldagrim somewhere, and I've heard rumours that a place called Phasmatys used to be good for that type of thing. 'Fore they all died, of course.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Bittercap mushrooms can only be grown in a special patch in Morytania, near the Mort Myre swamp. There the ground is especially dank and suited to growing poisonous fungii.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: There is a special patch for growing Belladonna - I believe that it is somewhere near Draynor Manor, where the ground is a tad 'unblessed'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you sell me something?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: That depends on whether I have it to sell. What is it that you're looking for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some plant cure.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Plant cure, eh? I might have some put aside for myself. Tell you what, I'll sell you some plant cure for 25 coins if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that sounds like a fair price.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I can get that much cheaper elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A bucket of compost.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: A bucket of compost, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 35 coins if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A rake.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: A rake, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 15 coins if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A plant pot.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Eh, I suppose I can spare you a plant pot, but I'll want 40 coins for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A watering can.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: A watering can, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 25 coins if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A gardening trowel.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: A gardening trowel, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 15 coins if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A seed dibber.

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: A seed dibber, eh? I might have one spare... tell you what, I'll sell it to you for 15 coins if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it, you don't have anything I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another time.

sections.title: Dialogue when using the pay option

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: Which patch would you like to pay protection for?

lines.speaker: Alan
lines.text: I want [payment] for that.
